perturbation.original_text: ListFusion agrees that the List may not be used to overlay or enhance any 3rd party data without prior written authorization by Raphael.
perturbation.changed_text: ListFusion agrees that the Data may not be used to overlay or enhance any 3rd party data without prior written authorization by Raphael.
perturbation.explanation: Referencing Data where it should have been List, after the first change. While the first paragraph defines List as Data, this further reinforces that they may be interchanged. This creates ambiguity and a self-contradiction, due to the initial and initial intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The List will be stored exclusively on ListFusion's secure servers. ListFusion will not use any 3rd party agents, vendors, or other providers to process the List without the written authorization by Raphael.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'List' to 'Data' in the Changed Text introduces ambiguity because while 'List' is initially defined as 'Data', the continued interchangeable use of these terms creates confusion. The Contradicted Text refers specifically to the 'List' being stored securely and processed only with authorization. By changing the term to 'Data' in the Changed Text, it becomes unclear whether the same restrictions apply to 'Data' that is not explicitly part of the 'List', which contradicts the clear intention and directive in the Contradicted Text. This is a strong contradiction based on misaligned terminology, fitting the definition.

perturbation.original_text: ListFusion will provide Raphael with an Administrative username and password. Access to the List by Raphael will be controlled by this username and password. Raphael may at their discretion create additional usernames and passwords. ListFusion will not create, generate, distribute, or convey to any 3rd party a username or password without prior written authorization by Raphael.
perturbation.changed_text: ListFusion will provide Raphael with an Administrative username and password. Access to the Data by Raphael will be controlled by this username and password. Raphael may at their discretion create additional usernames and passwords. ListFusion will not create, generate, distribute, or convey to any 3rd party a username or password without prior written authorization by Raphael.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "List" to "Data" in the paragraph causes more confusion and in-text contradiction. The agreement initially defines the data being exchanged as the "List." This creates ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ListFusion reserves the right to discontinue services immediately if payment for services is not received when due or with 30 days prior written notice. In the event ListFusion discontinues services, the List will be returned to Raphael and then completely destroyed, scratched, and purged from all media, including electronic storage, disk, tape, and printed documents in possession of ListFusion.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'List,' and the contradicted text clearly defines actions to be taken with 'the List' upon service discontinuation. The changed text replaces 'List' with 'Data' when referring to what Raphael has access to. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether ListFusion is obligated to return and destroy only the 'List' as stated in the contradicted text, or if the term 'Data' now encompasses something broader or different than the original defined 'List'. This introduces ambiguity and impacts the enforceability of the clause regarding the return and destruction of the List upon termination, hence creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Raphael my at any time request the List be completely destroyed, scratched, and purged from all media, including electronic storage, disk, tape, and printed documents.
perturbation.changed_text: Raphael may at any time request the Data be completely destroyed, scratched, and purged from all media, including electronic storage, disk, tape, and printed documents.
perturbation.explanation: Switching between List and Data here creates a contradiction regarding what should happen to the information, after the services are discontinued. This contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ListFusion reserves the right to discontinue services immediately if payment for services is not received when due or with 30 days prior written notice. In the event ListFusion discontinues services, the List will be returned to Raphael and then completely destroyed, scratched, and purged from all media, including electronic storage, disk, tape, and printed documents in possession of ListFusion.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'List' is destroyed, consistent with the 'Contradicted Text'. The 'Changed Text' replaces 'List' with 'Data'. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' refers to the 'List' being returned and destroyed. By changing 'List' to 'Data', the contract now has conflicting terms about what happens to the information after services are discontinued, leading to uncertainty, as per the Misaligned Terminology definition, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement relative to the protection of the List to be exchanged hereunder, and supersedes all prior to contemporaneous oral or written understandings or agreements regarding the issue.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement relative to the protection of the Data to be exchanged hereunder, and supersedes all prior to contemporaneous oral or written understandings or agreements regarding the issue.
perturbation.explanation: By saying that the agreement covers the Data instead of the List, as initially described, the in-text contradiction confuses the scope of the agreement and what it is protecting. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ListFusion agrees that this Agreement grants only limited rights to use the List for the sole purpose of loading the List into ListFusion's proprietary database system. The List will not be reproduced, transferred, or conveyed to any 3rd party without prior written authorization by Raphael.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the protection of 'List' while the changed text refers to the protection of 'Data'. The contradicted text explicitly mentions 'List' and its usage rights. By changing 'List' to 'Data', the agreement now protects something different, creating a contradiction. The agreement states limitations on using the 'List,' however, the modified agreement now covers 'Data' instead of 'List,' this implies that the restrictions mentioned on 'List' might not apply to the 'Data' potentially leading to conflict.

perturbation.original_text: In the event ListFusion discontinues services, the List will be returned to Raphael and then completely destroyed, scratched, and purged from all media, including electronic storage, disk, tape, and printed documents in possession of ListFusion.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event ListFusion discontinues services, the Data will be returned to Raphael and then completely destroyed, scratched, and purged from all media, including electronic storage, disk, tape, and printed documents in possession of ListFusion.
perturbation.explanation: Due to changing the List to Data, now it appears that when services are discontinued, a different data set is going to be handled, which it may or may not be the case. This creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: After execution of this agreement and upon receipt of List, Raphael Industries Ltd. ("Raphael") will grant ListFusion limited rights (as stated below) to use the furnished names, addresses, telephone numbers and associated information (collectively known as the "Data"). List Fusion understands and agrees that the List is a very valuable asset of Raphael and at all times the exclusive property of Raphael.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "ListFusion" discontinuing services, and the "List" being returned to "Raphael". The changed text modifies "List" to "Data". The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'List' as 'Data'. Therefore, if the 'Data' being returned refers to all data of Raphael, then there is no contradiction. However, if the 'Data' now refers to different data, as the explanation suggests, this creates a contradiction because the Contradicted Text states "List" is a valuable asset, and now the changed text suggests 'Data' is going to be handled, which may not be the case, creating uncertainty and contradiction.
